prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a small explorer robot stands in thick swirling fog, holding a softly glowing lantern that lights only a small bubble around it while several faint ghostly shapes of possible rooms hover unseen in the haze, the robot tilting its head thoughtfully rather than panicking. Style: friendly XKCD or Kurzgesagt feel, expressive posture, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: An explorer robot in dense fog lights a small bubble with a lantern while faint ghostly possible rooms float unseen around it, depicting a hidden Markov state where the agent perceives only a sliver of the true world.
caption: When the true state hides in fog, an agent never sees where it is; it only ever holds a glowing guess about where it might be.

after_heading: 3. The Belief State and the Bayes-Filter Update
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a calm detective robot facing a lineup of several translucent suspect silhouettes of different sizes, with a fresh clue floating in as a glowing puff that makes some silhouettes swell brighter and others shrink and fade, the robot adjusting their relative sizes with a gentle gesture. Style: friendly XKCD or Kurzgesagt feel, expressive, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A detective robot resizes translucent suspect silhouettes as a new glowing clue arrives, brightening likely candidates and fading unlikely ones, illustrating how each observation reweights the belief distribution in a Bayes filter update.
caption: Each new observation is a clue that does not reveal the answer; it just reweights how strongly you believe in each remaining possibility.

prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a tidy robot tiling a smoothly curved hill with just a handful of flat rigid boards, each board resting on a chosen pin in the ground so the boards together hug the curve closely enough, leaving most of the hill untouched while the robot looks pleased with the efficient shortcut. Style: friendly XKCD or Kurzgesagt feel, expressive, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot approximates a smoothly curved hill using only a few flat boards pinned at selected points, capturing the curve without covering every spot, illustrating how point-based value iteration approximates the belief value function from a small set of belief points.
caption: You do not need a plane for every belief; a few well placed flat pieces, chosen at the beliefs that actually come up, hug the true value curve closely enough.

after_heading: 4. When Learned Memory Beats Explicit Belief, and When It Does Not
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a cheerful robot with a completely smooth blank head and no memory at all, striding confidently into the very same shallow pit it has clearly fallen into before, identical robot footprints leading in and out of the same pit in a loop, the robot smiling obliviously as if seeing the spot for the first time. Style: friendly XKCD or Kurzgesagt feel, dry knowing humor, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A blank headed memoryless robot cheerfully walks into the same pit it already fell into, with looping footprints showing the repeated mistake, illustrating how an agent without memory cannot distinguish identical observations and repeats errors a belief state would avoid.
caption: An agent with no memory treats every identical view as brand new, so it walks into the same trap again and again; remembering the past is what turns observations into a usable state.
